title: CostStore hardening: package export, double-open guard, type fixes, test coverage
analysis: CostStore (shared/cost_store.py) is a working async SQLite writer but has gaps identified in task 304 review: (1) not exported from the shared package __init__.py despite other modules (cli_invoke, config_models, usage_gate) following that pattern, (2) open() silently overwrites _conn if called twice (leaking the first connection), (3) __aexit__ uses *exc: Any catch-all instead of the standard 3-arg signature, (4) _execute types params as bare tuple instead of tuple[Any, ...], (5) test_public_api.py doesn't include cost_store in union/module checks. Existing tests cover init, open/close, context manager, not-opened guards, save operations, and connection reuse — but miss double-open, persist-across-reopen, and post-close scenarios. All changes are confined to 4 files in the shared subproject.

steps.description: Write failing test_cost_store_all in TestModuleLevelAll (test_public_api.py): assert cost_store.__all__ == {'CostStore'}. Also update test_init_all_covers_all_module_symbols to include cost_store in the union, and test_no_private_symbols_in_any_all to include cost_store. These tests will fail because CostStore is not yet exported from shared/__init__.py.
Add 'from shared.cost_store import CostStore' and 'CostStore' to __all__ in shared/__init__.py. Also add CostStore to test_top_level_imports. This makes step-1 tests pass.

Write failing test_double_open_raises in TestCostStoreOpenClose (test_cost_store.py): open() → open() again → assert RuntimeError('CostStore already opened'). Currently open() silently overwrites _conn, so this will fail.
Add double-open guard to CostStore.open(): at method entry, if self._conn is not None raise RuntimeError('CostStore already opened'). This makes step-3 test pass.

Write failing post-close guard tests in new TestPostCloseGuard class (test_cost_store.py): (a) open → close → save_invocation → assert RuntimeError('CostStore not opened'), (b) open → close → save_account_event → assert RuntimeError('CostStore not opened'). These should already pass due to existing _require_conn guard, but formalizes the contract — write them as explicit regression tests.

Write persist-across-reopen test in new TestPersistAcrossReopen class (test_cost_store.py): open → save_invocation → close → open again (same path) → raw SELECT verifies row exists. This validates data survives close/reopen cycle. Should pass with current impl once double-open guard is in place (close sets _conn=None, so reopen is allowed).

Fix __aexit__ signature from (*exc: Any) to standard 3-arg form: (self, exc_type: type[BaseException] | None, exc_val: BaseException | None, exc_tb: object) -> None. Pure type-safety fix, no behavioral change.
Fix _execute params type from bare 'tuple' to 'tuple[Any, ...]'. Pure type-safety fix, no behavioral change.

design_decisions.decision: Double-open raises RuntimeError rather than silently closing and reopening
design_decisions.rationale: A double-open is always a bug — silently recovering would mask lifecycle errors. Consistent with _require_conn pattern which also raises RuntimeError for misuse.
Avoids importing types.TracebackType. The 'object' type is sufficient since the parameter is unused (just forwarded to close()). This matches what pyright accepts without additional imports.

design_decisions.decision: Post-close guard tests written even though they pass immediately
design_decisions.rationale: These are regression tests for a contract: after close(), writes must raise. Without explicit tests, someone could refactor _require_conn and break this invariant without detection.

design_decisions.decision: Steps 7-8 (type fixes) grouped at end without separate test steps
design_decisions.rationale: These are pure signature/type annotation changes with zero behavioral change. No new tests needed — existing tests already exercise the methods. Pyright/mypy would catch regressions.

reuse.what: __all__ export pattern from cli_invoke, config_models, usage_gate
reuse.how: Add 'from shared.cost_store import CostStore' following same import style, add 'CostStore' to __all__ list
Add test_cost_store_all method following same assert set(module.__all__) == {...} pattern
Add cost_store import and include in union calculation and no-private-symbols check
Same RuntimeError pattern for double-open: if self._conn is not None: raise RuntimeError('CostStore already opened')

reuse.what: Existing test fixtures (tmp_path, pytest.raises, async context manager patterns)
reuse.how: New test classes follow same @pytest.mark.asyncio class pattern, use tmp_path for DB, pytest.raises for error assertions
